headline: Sudan floor confirmation with 'abandoned crisis' notation. UN designated Sudan 'world's largest displacement crisis' (14M displaced); UN official described as 'abandoned crisis' — new institutional framing within window. $2.8B humanitarian appeal 16% funded. At least 3 children per day dying in El-Fasher; 25M+ acutely food insecure. Sudan war fourth-year anniversary reached April 15. Floor maintained.
key_evidence: UN designation: 'world's largest displacement crisis' (14M displaced).
UN official characterization: 'abandoned crisis' — new institutional framing this cycle.
At least 3 children per day dying in El-Fasher due to food shortages (Sudan Doctors Network).
RSF intensifying attack on El-Fasher; Port Sudan drone assault on aid gateway previously documented.

evidence.finding: UN designation as world's largest displacement crisis; Sudan war fourth year.

evidence.source: OCHA Sudan Humanitarian Needs and Response Plan April 2026

scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 unchanged (floor). El-Fasher children's deaths sustain floor-level EQ2 Priority for Vulnerable.

abandoned_crisis_notation: The 'abandoned crisis' designation by a UN official is a new institutional framing within window. This is documented for cross-cycle continuity but does not move the composite below floor.
negativeSignals: World's largest displacement crisis; 'abandoned crisis' framing; 16% appeal funded; 3+ children per day dying in El-Fasher; 25M+ food insecure; RSF intensification.
sector_context: Sudan, South Sudan, Myanmar comprise the conflict/humanitarian floor cluster with active in-window negative evidence. Sudan distinguished by 'abandoned crisis' framing and the 16% appeal-funding gap.
open_actions_resolved: 'Abandoned crisis' designation captured as new institutional notation
16% appeal-funding gap captured as new in-window indicator
open_actions_carried: Floor exit criteria not in trajectory
